Usually, the way the Instagram platform works on your local market, I would say start posting in the morning, lunch, 3 PM, 6 PM, and before going to bed. But Lisa goes in the Instagram at night and doesn't go in the morning. So you posting more is going to help you be in front of them more times, which means more attention for you and more engagement. Cool. Now, networking. This is kind of like the gasoline on the fire. And one of the most important things that you need to do, how it works and the reason behind the algorithm. Now, let me tell you something about how the Instagram algorithm works. And this took me four or five years to find this out. So the Instagram algorithm is all determined by engagement, like I said at the beginning. And this way you gamify this is by getting big pages to like and comment on your stuff. Why? There's two reasons why. First off, we want to get, let's say, 100,000 follower pages, or 10,000 follower pages in fitness to like and comment on your stuff. Why? Because it tells Instagram first that an account with high authority just liked and commented on your picture, which means they're like, OK, this content is great, so I'm going to show you two more people. Why? Because the only way Instagram makes money is by people keeping people on the platform. And the way they keep people on the platform is with content that Instagram think is relevant to that audience. So the way you gamify this is by getting a big page to like and comment on your stuff, because it tells Instagram, oh, a big page is like and comment on this. That means this is actually good, so I'm going to show you two more people. That's the first ripple effect. The second one is that when that account likes and comment on your stuff, if they're in the same niche, ideally, we already know that that audience is interested in our stuff. So when they like and comment on that stuff, that audience is also going to see an index board feed of their phones. And because they're in the same industry, same niche, they're going to also like, comment, follow you. So that's kind of like the idea. Now, what you do it in your local area is you get as many local influencers liking and commenting on your pictures to make sure you put them and you rank in your local area. You'll basically rank all your competitors by doing this thing alone. So what you want to do is do your market research. How do you actually find the local influencers in your space? And this is kind of like what took me four or five years to figure out. I was no successful. And then like two years ago, everything blew up. And this is what? Market research. Now, doing this will double triple your engagement literally overnight. Get your posts in the areas it wouldn't normally be seen creating major growth. And hack the Instagram algorithm and make it work for you rather than against you. Now, how do you actually find local influencers or the gyms, right, necessarily in your area? I just, in that time, 100,000 followers account, I did exactly the same thing. It got 14,000 likes and 113 comments. So I had almost similar results because I just modeled success. And again, that's called repurposing content. All you got to do is take a screenshot, put it on your account, and give credit to the original creator. Why? That's powerful because it will create a sense of community on your page. It's not only about you anymore. Like Alex was saying, it's not about you. It's about the community. So if you're able to just repurpose content, give credit to the original creator, everybody loves free promotion. Everybody loves that. So you're giving credit to them. They're happy. You're providing content to your audience. They're happy. And you're growing your page. So you're happy. And the next thing is a win, win, win for everybody. And it saves time for you as well. So how to become relevant in your place is with attention. Transformation pictures work extremely, extremely well. And this will, again, create a sense of community and bring more people in. If they will see that it's not about you anymore, it's not about the gym owners, not about all you guys that are fricking jacked, it's all about them. And what people see is not about them. They're going to be like, man, if Lisa had this amazing transformation picture, I want to also be part of it. If Johnny got fricking jacked and became Thor, like, I don't know, Thor somewhere there, they're going to do it as well. So you don't have to create all the content yourself. This is one of the major things that I see a lot of people struggling with is that, man, OK, I get it. You've got to create content. You've got to do all these things. But content takes time. Again, repurpose content, leverage other people's content for your own good. Everybody wins. And you can also start doing infographics. They follow you, they turn post notifications on, and every single time you post, you're there on their phones. Now, get people going on IG if they already have it, and following either. Most people that go to the gym, they have you heard the saying that if you didn't lift, if they didn't go on the gram, right? If they didn't go on the gram, it didn't happen. So people go on Instagram, to your gym, take pictures, and then leave. I've seen that way too many times. But the point is that people are taking pictures and posting them on Instagram. So why not just encourage that behavior? So tell your trainers and your employees to encourage people to take pictures and post them on Instagram, and you will feature them. So just tell them, hey, use hashtag, whatever your gym is called, and tag us, and you will feature them there. People like recognition, and like, you know, they say, hey, you did a great job. Now, it's alivious to pay for you to have and create content over and over again, because again, it's paying the butt to do it. And it also tells your trainers to do the same thing, and this process, both on their accounts as well. So you want to create like this mini little army with your trainers to do it. And you want them to become active advocates of your gym and brand. If you're like, if your trainers or your employees, whatever, that are training in a facility like your clients are not doing this, tell them, hey, you should probably post pictures on Instagram, and tell them, hey, you should come train with Johnny, that's super awesome. And to help Lisa lose 100 pounds. So it would start driving more traffic, because you're having this little mini army of people that are also promoting for you for free. Now, Instagram is first a branding plate, and then selling. So this is one of the major things, like I said, I showed you some examples with BioFit. They were trying to sell way too hard, but the branding part was sucked. So do not try to sell right from the get, create massive brand awareness, and then sell. The sell will come naturally. Again, the fitness industry is probably one of the easiest ones to capitalize on in, because the product or the result is extremely visual. So you don't even have to sell. You just have to post like a guy with a freaking abs, and they're like, oh my God, I want that. Or like girl with a big booty or like a squat booty, and like, oh my God, I want that. So you don't even have to sell them. It just sells itself. Cool. So again, now you know how to create awareness. How do you actually sell? And the Bio is the easiest place to sell on. And this is what I would recommend in your case, specifically for a local market. What we do and who we are, our mission. That's the first bullet point. The second one is what results we get for people. Third one, DM us, X word to get X results, and then a call to action, whatever it is. This is an example right here. And use Instagram Stories to sell and have people coming. Instagram Stories is where the money's at. I'm gonna show you how. Now, how to use Instagram Stories effectively is by providing value. What to do, where it's not to do. Now, how many people here use Instagram Stories under gyms? Okay, now many people. Everybody else who didn't raise their hand start using them like right now, almost. But how you actually use Instagram Stories is not by just, hey, I'm here at my gym and today we're gonna work out. That's not how you do it. Nobody cares about your life. Nobody cares about your dog. Nobody cares about what you ate. They care about the value you're providing to them. So instead of just vlogging about your day, take an analytical approach and just a better approach. So do not show behind the scenes what you do or what you ate for lunch. Nobody cares. Show tips and tricks of what results you can get for people. Have a clear call to action and write whatever you say via sound. Let's say for example you say, hey, three tips on how to lose weight. Right, three tips on how to lose weight. Why? Because people not always have their sound turned on on their phones. And most of the time they're just reading and if they have a clear text and they're like, oh, three tips on how to lose weight, then they're gonna turn on the value. So, and use hashtags and geolocations on your stories because that is extremely, extremely simple to rank. And again, on Instagram stories, use more videos and less pictures. Pictures last like three seconds, videos last 15 seconds. You're able to get an undivided attention for 15 seconds at a time from them, right? So again, this was a story that I did last week actually. Again, show your face behind the brand. Now, viral local giveaway strategy. Now, this, I'm going to walk you over through a strategy that I said at the beginning that we generate about $700, at least at about $80,000 in revenue in about 48-hour period with 1K ad spend. This was for a local beauty shop in Canada. Now, giveaways work extremely, extremely well for any niche and any market, and specifically in the fitness space. We already know how well the six-week challenge works. Now, you need to understand the basics of what we cover first before I go into this in order for you to capitalize on this. You know content, you know market research, you know your local influencers. Now we've got those three down. We're going to go dive into the giveaway. So the first thing is that in order to execute a good giveaway, these things cannot just be done out of the blue for no reason at all. So people see through this, believe it or not. If you just do a giveaway for the second group doing a giveaway, you start doing over and over again, people will see right through it. So have a reason. Valentine's Day, July 4th, back to summer. I don't know. Your birthday, Thanksgiving, Black Friday, Christmas, New Year's, whatever it is, just do a giveaway, but with a really good pre-frame and a reason behind it. If you're going to do something super, super massive, make sure you do it just a few times a year, and not just every single month. Because then people, again, will get bored and say, oh, I'm not going to participate in this one. They're going to do one next month. So urgency and scarcity are your biggest place right here. Now, there's something that can be done in advance that cannot be just done in 24 hours. This needs a little bit of planning. So what I'm going to show you right now takes a little bit of work upfront, but is extremely, extremely profitable. Now, numbers one, this was done, the campaign that we did for the beauty shop in Canada. So we had about 75% opt-in rate on the landing page. We had 10,000 profile views locally, and that means a huge retargeting opportunity for you in your local market. And everybody who participated also got 30% to 40%. Even if they didn't win, we followed up with those leads and got 30% to 40%. This encouraged people to come back to your place, too, in that case, the beauty shop to that place and spend money just because they get a 30% to 40% discount. And it feels super good to them because even if they didn't win, they're getting something for free still, just for participating. And then you can upsell those clients extremely, extremely easy because they already come into your doors. Now, we have done about 630 leads from that and about 48 to 72 hour window. Window and the yearly lifetime value of the customer was about $420. So about 30% closed on the phone, and that means almost $80,000 in revenue. We got over 700 followers on Instagram from that campaign. They were local, and the total cost of the campaign was less than $1,000. And this was to get several local influencers and push traffic from bigger ones in the niche, even though they were not necessarily in those areas.
